programme.desc: The life and career of the actor known for roles in films including Taxi Driver, Thelma & Louise, Bad Lieutenant, Sister Act, Reservoir Dogs, Cop Land and The Irishman.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of Ron Howard, whose films include Splash, Cocoon, Apollo 13, Frost/Nixon, Cinderella Man, Rush, and A Beautiful Mind, for which he won two Oscars.

programme.title: Vegas: The Story of Sin City
programme.desc: A look at how Las Vegas became farther removed from national attitudes, with Elvis Presley's comeback helping cement the city as an international entertainment destination.

programme.title: The Alien Autopsy Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of two music entrepreneurs in the mid-1990s who claimed to have unearthed top-secret footage showing the corpse of an alien recovered from a 1947 crash site near Roswell, New Mexico. They set out to show it to the world, but first they had to find the money to buy it from the ex-military cameraman who holds the film reels.

programme.title: History of the Sitcom
programme.desc: The topic of upward mobility in situation comedies, with many shows helping Americans come to grips with their place in a supposedly classless society.

programme.title: Paul Simon: In Restless Dreams
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary exploring the 60-year career of the singer-songwriter, looking at his return to the studio to record his 2023 album Seven Psalms.

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: The police discover the bodies at the condo on Bundy Drive and the trial of OJ Simpson for the murders of Nicole Brown Simpson and Ronald Goldman gets under way.

programme.title: I Am Patrick Swayze
programme.desc: A tribute to the American actor, known for his roles in hits like Dirty Dancing, Ghost and The Outsiders. With contributions by Demi Moore and Rob Lowe among others.

programme.title: My Icon: Leon Pryce
programme.desc: The former Bradford Bulls and St Helens star discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and sporting career.

programme.title: Katie Price: Nothing to Hide
programme.desc: As Katie and Peter Andre become a celebrity power couple, a carefully orchestrated rebrand kills off Jordan, threatening her identity, relationship and future.

programme.title: Nothing Compares
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Sinéad O'Connor's rise to worldwide fame, and how her iconoclastic personality resulted in her exile from the pop mainstream.

programme.title: OJ: Made in America
programme.desc: The police discover the bodies at the condo on Bundy Drive and the trial of OJ Simpson for the murders of Nicole Brown Simpson and Ronald Goldman gets under way.

programme.title: Land of Primates
programme.desc: As humans take up more space, dislodged baboons must adapt to survive. This episode reveals how they travel across an ever-changing African landscape.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: The work of the staff at an African game reserve. The rangers at Ol Pejeta Conservancy in Kenya face a difficult decision when two critically threatened black rhinos brutally attack each other.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: The rangers face a challenge as they undergo a mass relocation of an endangered species of hartebeest from a game reserve to Ol Pejeta.

programme.title: Slovenia: Where Nature Comes First
programme.desc: The natural wonders of Slovenia, from Mediterranean olive groves to snow-capped peaks, as well as wildlife such as brown bears and the world's largest trout.

programme.title: Extreme Animals: One Wild Day
programme.desc: A look at how animals adapt to the extremes of the desert, including an epic journey of desert elephants and a beetle that enjoys morning yoga.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: The Vredefort Dome in South Africa, which was created by a meteorite collision two billion years ago and provides a unique natural haven.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The coast of Mozambique is known for its giants, from visiting humpbacks to the resident whale sharks. But just as important are the water's tiny creatures.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: When the conservation team finds evidence of poachers in the area, an attempt is made to move two white rhinos to Ol Pejeta.

programme.title: Ol Pejeta Diaries
programme.desc: With elephant numbers on the rise, Ol Pejeta must deal with new conflicts, such as one group which is going around raiding crops.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: There is a noisy welcome for spider monkeys Hickory, Flint and Pumpkin, and love is in the air for the flirtatious capuchins. Plus, big girl Oshine shares a giant ice lolly.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Woolly monkey Sara undergoes an emergency caesarean. Golden-cheeked gibbon Teo takes his first wobbly steps 20 metres high and Winslow's capuchins go on a treasure hunt.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: Hidden within the Kalahari Desert and pulsating with natural beauty, the Okavango attracts scores of animals to its lush waters.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: A look at how plankton, the smallest of creatures, underpins the ocean's food chain, meaning that without it, marine life as it is known would cease to exist.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: The documentary introduces a cheetah cub called Usana. Born with the odds stacked against her, few cheetah cubs make it through their first year. Luckily for Usana, she has a mother who will stop at nothing to keep her cubs safe. Guiding her through her first months, Usana's mother instils a new hope for her kind and Usana might just follow in her footsteps.

programme.title: Shark with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist learns about the sharks of the Indian Ocean, which despite finding ways to adapt to the environment, face threats from climate change, pollution and overfishing.

programme.title: Africa's Claws and Jaws
programme.desc: Sharks are among the planet's oldest and most highly evolved predators. This documentary follows the people dedicated to learning more about these creatures and their plight.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Vermont family finally finds their dream spot on Lake Champlain.

programme.desc: When an expat returns to her Georgia hometown to find a lakeside retreat for her and her three young sons, a longtime friend joins her to help find the right property and spearhead the renovations needed to transform it into her perfect cabin.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys travel to Patrick, S.C., to build a huge wedding pavilion, the biggest timber frame they've ever created; on a job this big, they need some extra help so they bring along Mark's latest hire and have fun teaching the new kid.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders head to the hills of Pocahontas County, W.V., to try something new; working with repurposed factory beams and hand hewn timbers, they create a hybrid timber frame structure with a shed roof.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In New Ringgold, Pa., the crew members prepare to take down and move a carriage house; they find all sorts of treasures in the 150-year-old pioneer garage before they carefully strip it, lift the roof off in sections and disassemble the beams.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael find a charming 1930s bungalow close to the beach and jump at the opportunity to turn it into a vacation rental; with beach season approaching and a baby on the way, they must work hard and fast to get the house ready in time.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The oversized lot makes this 1920s home a great purchase, but Ashley and Michael must reverse a boring 80s renovation with the historic feel of handcrafted doors, shiplap ceilings and original floors that get a fun checkerboard pattern.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays turn an alley home once filled with cats into a getaway; this Galveston gem is highlighted with custom features including reclaimed wood walls and an upcycled stock tank bathtub.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Camelbacks are not common in Galveston, Texas, but the challenge is to make this one a historic head-turner; the Cordrays transform the attic into a cozy family space and build an outdoor sofa that a Gulf storm can't destroy.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays look to balance function with history as they tackle a living room, bathroom and dining room remodel for a young couple and their three children in their 1896 Victorian home in Galveston, Texas.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays help a first-time homeowner bring back character to the 1940s home she describes as her Millennial Mansion; with features like a reclaimed brick porch, custom woodwork inside and a colorful new kitchen, it's a complete transformation.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Professional tree-house builder Michael "Murph" Murphy wants to build a cottage retreat for his family in Washington; he enlists his friends to create natural siding and a massive fishing net for lounging above the living space.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A couple wants to build a country cottage on their remote family farm in southeast Georgia despite the fact that building that kind of structure in four months will be anything but easy; the novice builders face material delays, hurricanes and wildlife.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A young cabin builder creates a one-of-a-kind cabin in the Catskill Mountains in upstate New York during the winter months; because of the new and complicated design, the crew must adapt to the environment.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: An Indiana farming family combines old with new to create a grain bin retreat on their busy working farm.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A couple plans to build their home out of straw bales in the Arizona desert in only six months; using Earthship architecture as their motivation, they hope to make this structure modern, durable and unique.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Naturalist builder Cat Taylor builds a remote sanctuary in the piney woods of Northwest Texas; she builds the two-story house using a cob mixture and adds touches of comfort to entertain family members.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: From the edge of an active volcano in Hawaii and the Appalachian Mountains to the deserts of New Mexico and upstate New York, builders battle harsh conditions to construct innovative, self-sufficient homes in remote areas.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A surprise ant invasion, a 1960s game show, and a basketball showdown keeps the team on their toes as they take on a fifth generation cabin, adding bathroom privacy and a cozy nap nook.
